message.content:
For each of the following files, add the import line `import PageHeader from '../components/layout/PageHeader';` if it doesn't already exist. Add it right after the last import statement in each file. Only add if the import doesn't already exist.

Files to update (all in C:/Users/User/citedby-ai/pages/):
- Account.tsx
- Affiliation.tsx
- AiCitationSimulator.tsx
- AiMonitoring.tsx
- AiResponseLayer.tsx
- AlertsAEO.tsx
- AnswerFirstAudit.tsx
- ApiDeveloper.tsx
- BlogOptimizer.tsx
- BotTracking.tsx
- Clients.tsx
- ContentWriter.tsx
- Directory.tsx
- EeatBuilder.tsx
- Integrations.tsx
- LlmsTxtGenerator.tsx
- Messages.tsx
- MissionOrders.tsx
- Recharge.tsx
- Reputation.tsx
- SMCStudio.tsx
- SourceOfTruth.tsx

For each file, use sed to add the import after the last 'from' import line if it doesn't already exist. Use a simple check with grep first.

Use this pattern for each file:
```
grep -q "PageHeader" FILE || sed -i '/^import.*from/!b;:a;n;/^import.*from/ba;i\import PageHeader from '\''../components/layout/PageHeader'\'';' FILE
```

Actually, a simpler approach: for each file, check if it has the import, and if not, add it after the line with the component's interface declaration (which comes after all imports). Use this simpler approach:

For each file, if grep doesn't find "PageHeader", add the import line at line 2 position (after the first import line of React).

Actually, the simplest reliable approach: for each file, check with grep, and if not found, use sed to insert after the first `from 'lucide-react';` line.

Do this for all files listed above.

content.text: I'll take a more reliable approach: first check each file for the import, then find the last import line number and insert after it. Let me start by checking which files already have the import.

input.command: cd C:/Users/User/citedby-ai/pages && for f in Account.tsx Affiliation.tsx AiCitationSimulator.tsx AiMonitoring.tsx AiResponseLayer.tsx AlertsAEO.tsx AnswerFirstAudit.tsx ApiDeveloper.tsx BlogOptimizer.tsx BotTracking.tsx Clients.tsx ContentWriter.tsx Directory.tsx EeatBuilder.tsx Integrations.tsx LlmsTxtGenerator.tsx Messages.tsx MissionOrders.tsx Recharge.tsx Reputation.tsx SMCStudio.tsx SourceOfTruth.tsx; do if grep -q "PageHeader" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then echo "SKIP: $f (already has PageHeader)"; else echo "NEED: $f"; fi; done
input.description: Check which files already have PageHeader import
